titleStmt.title: Telegram from Richard and Eulalia Berridge to Lady Clonbrock, 18 July 1916
Letters 1916-1923
titleStmt.author: Richard Berridge

editionStmt.p: This work was originally published by Maynooth University in Ireland in 2017. In 2026 this data, stored in a relational database was extracted and converted into this TEI/XML document.

availability.p: This is an open access work licensed under Creative Commons Attribution NonCommercial 4.0 International license (CC BY-NC 4.0).

note.p:
The telegram was sent to congratulate Augusta Caroline Dillon (née Crofton), Lady Clonbrock (1840-1928), and her husband, Luke Gerald Dillon, 4th Baron Clonbrock (1834-1917), on the occasion of their golden wedding anniversary in July 1916. Lady Clonbrock was also a prominent member of the Irish Women's Association, founded to provide aid and assistance to Irish soldiers and prisoners of war. The telegram was sent by Dick and Enlalia Berridge.
Richard Berridge (1871-1941) was the owner of Ballynahinch Castle in Co. Galway. Moving up from the position of High Sheriff Berridge served as Deputy Lieutenant in addition to fighting in the First World War as a Lieutenant with the Royal Naval Volunteer Reserves.
Mary Eulalia Berridge, née Lesley, (1881-1957), was American born. In 1905 she married Richard Berridge and went on to serve as President of the Roundstone branch of the United Irishwomen. This organisation sought to assist men with agricultural pursuits in order to further the progress of rural economy.

msIdentifier.collection: Clonbrock Estate Papers, Collection List No 54; Ms. 35,793 (9)

projectDesc.p: The data in these XML files was generated based on a data dump from the Magellan database (https://github.com/Maynooth-Center-for-Digital-Humanities/Magellan). Each database record has been converted to a JSON file (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/json). The element section of the JSON file contains the TEI-encoded contents of the letters. The TEI XML has been cleaned and made well-formed using a Python script (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/src).

body.ab:
Dublin T
Handed in at} 6.0pm Received here at} 7.44pm To Lady Clonbrock 10 Cadogan Gdns
Ldn SW Greetings and all good wishes
Dick and Eulalia Berridge
TELEGRAM.
NO CHARGE FOR DELIVERY. Berridge Lady Clonbrock
10 Cadogan Gdns
